top_categories.workout_context: Tour of the Peak's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 113 miles.
84ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Tour of the Peak's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
At Tour of the Peak, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
At mile 60 of 113, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
9,500ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Tour of the Peak. Train it until it feels boring.
9,500ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Tour of the Peak, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 113 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Low-cadence force work for 9,500ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Tour of the Peak’s 113 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Tour of the Peak. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Highly technical terrain at Tour of the Peak demands practice on similar surfaces. Ride mixed terrain at race-day cadence weekly. Practice cornering, descending, and power delivery on unstable surfaces. Dial in tire pressure before race week — 5 PSI wrong costs you 15+ minutes over 113 miles.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, TT Threshold, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 113 miles of mixed terrain in Bakewell, Derbyshire, England.
